hypothesis: Enhancing SSTR2 selectivity by optimizing charge complementarity at position 6 (currently NICKWFFWKTFTSC) and increasing steric discrimination at position 11 to better engage SSTR2-specific ECL2/ECL3/TM5/TM6 regions while reducing off-target affinity.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current design strategy is not sufficiently disrupting off-target interactions at non-pharmacophore positions. A focused redesign of positions 1, 2, 5, 6, 11, and 12 with mutations that reduce SSTR3/SSTR5 contacts is needed to improve selectivity.

proposed_changes.rationale: These positions are non-pharmacophore and are known to contribute to off-target interactions (especially with SSTR3/SSTR5). Focusing design on these positions can help disrupt off-target contacts while preserving the pharmacophore (positions 7-10 and Cys3/Cys14).
These mutations are selected to disrupt conserved pocket interactions with SSTR3/SSTR5 while maintaining flexibility and compatibility with the SSTR2 binding pocket.
